title: Call for Proposals: Open Data Fund for Central America 2025/2026
body: "Connecting Civil Society through Open Data: Strengthening the Capacities of Civil Society Actors in Central America  Financed by:   Magna Carta Fund for Human Rights and Democracy (MCF), Government of the United Kingdom   Duration:   September 15, 2025  to March 31, 2026   Budget:   $70,000 USD   Geographical scope:   Panama, Costa Rica, Guatemala and El Salvador   Convocatoria:   We are looking for an implementing organisation with regional experience to carry out the project. We invite eligible entities to submit proposals that strengthen citizen participation, transparency and digital inclusion.   Background  Open data is an essential tool for strengthening transparent and participatory governance. This project seeks to empower civil society organisations (CSOs) in Central America so they can access, interpret and use public data effectively.  Project objectives:   empower civil society with tools and training to to use public data in advocacy and oversight processes foster regional collaboration between organisations to build a resilient open data ecosystem promote inclusive participation of women, people with disabilities and marginalised communities support evidence-based advocacy to influence public policies and monitor government performance amplify impact through communication strategies and visibility effort   Focus areas   digital inclusion and connectivity gender equality disability inclusion climate resilience governance and citizen participation   Implementing partner profile:   organisation registered in Costa Rica, Guatemala or Panama (registration in three countries not required) proven capacity to operate regionally, including in El Salvador experience in open data, civic technology or digital rights financial stability for at least two months before the first disbursement strong commitment to gender equality and inclusion government agencies are not eligible   Key deliverables:   regional mapping and needs assessment tailored capacity development and training strategy delivery of inclusive training in at least four countries communication strategy to share results and promote open data final report with recommendations for future collaborations   Project timeline     Milestone Date     Launch of call for bids August 12, 2025   Deadline for submissions August 31, 2025   Preselection September 1 to 5, 2025   Due diligence September 8 to 19,  2025   Signing of MOU September 22 to26, 2025   Project start September 22to 26,  2025   Project end March 31, 2026   Final report June 2026     Evaluation criteria  Based on the criteria document, the following will be evaluated:   alignment with FCDO and SDG objectives technical feasibility and methodology regional scope and partnerships gender and inclusion strategy value for money and sustainability engagement with government institutions   How to apply  Proposals must be submitted by August 31 2025 (23:59 GMT) via this online form.  Terms of reference:  Terms of Reference: Call for proposals for implementing partner  (ODT, 201 KB) "
